title: coleataenia longifolia subsp. combsii
intro: Coleataenia longifolia subsp. combsii, also known as Combs' coleataenia or Combs' wild rye, is a subspecies of plant in the wild rye genus that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves, which contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Coleataenia longifolia subsp. combsii include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and stems are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties.
